response.justification: Maria has strong staff-level engineering fundamentals, TypeScript expertise, and recent direct experience with AI agent infrastructure (MCP frameworks at re:cinq). However, she lacks Python—essential for production AI/ML work—and her background shows no demonstrated experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLMs, or multi-agent systems beyond infrastructure tooling. The AI exposure is very recent (April 2025) and focused on developer tools rather than building production AI solutions. She could succeed if the MCP infrastructure role involved deeper model/pipeline work than the resume reflects, but the gaps are material.
strengths: Current experience building MCP frameworks for AI agent infrastructure directly aligns with job's key technology stack
Staff-level IC track record across multiple organizations with proven technical leadership and mentoring ability
Strong TypeScript/Node.js and cloud systems expertise (AWS CDK, microservices, infrastructure) applicable to production AI deployment
concerns: No Python experience listed despite it being a primary required language for AI/ML engineering at this level
No demonstrated expertise with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI)
Recent transition to AI work (April 2025) with unclear depth—MCP experience is infrastructure/tooling focused rather than building production AI solutions

key_factors.factor: MCP/AI agent infrastructure experience at re:cinq
No demonstrated RAG, vector DB, or LLM system building
